skill.name: cosmic-database
skill.description: Access COSMIC to download mutation datasets, query Cancer Gene Census, and retrieve mutational signatures when your genomic analysis requires curated somatic mutation resources.
skill.summary: Access curated COSMIC cancer mutation data, Cancer Gene Census, and mutational signatures for genomic analysis workflows.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Security audit evaluated 35 static findings from the code-generated scan. Of these, 33 are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting markdown formatting backticks as shell command execution and documentation text as cryptographic operations. The 2 confirmed findings (network requests via HTTP to cancer.sanger.ac.uk, and file writes to save downloaded data) are part of the skill's legitimate functionality for accessing the COSMIC cancer mutation database. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or credential theft patterns were found. The overall risk is LOW and the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Network access to external server
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP requests to cancer.sanger.ac.uk to download COSMIC database files. This is legitimate and intentional but requires network access. The connection uses HTTPS and standard authentication.

low_findings.title: File write to local filesystem
low_findings.description: The script writes downloaded COSMIC data files to the local filesystem. This is expected behavior for a data download utility. Files are written to the specified output directory.

low_findings.title: Base64 encoding for authentication
low_findings.description: The script uses base64 encoding for HTTP Basic Authentication headers when authenticating with the COSMIC server. This is standard practice and the actual security is provided by HTTPS transport layer encryption.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credentials passed as command-line arguments
dangerous_patterns.description: The download script accepts COSMIC email and password as positional command-line arguments (sys.argv). This exposes credentials in process listings and shell history. The SKILL.md example also shows credentials in code examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Missing SSL verification override
dangerous_patterns.description: The requests library defaults to SSL verification which is correct, but no explicit verify parameter is set and no certificate handling is configured. This is acceptable for connections to a legitimate HTTPS endpoint.

content.user_title: Access COSMIC for cancer mutation data analysis
content.value_statement: Researchers need trusted somatic mutation data for cancer genomics analysis but navigating COSMIC database APIs is complex and time-consuming. This skill provides a direct, packaged workflow to download mutation datasets, query the Cancer Gene Census, and retrieve mutational signatures for reproducible genomic research.
actual_capabilities: Download COSMIC mutation exports in TSV and VCF format for variant analysis
Query the Cancer Gene Census for curated lists of cancer-related genes
Retrieve mutational signatures including SBS, DBS, and ID types
Authenticate with COSMIC credentials for secure data access
Load and filter downloaded COSMIC data using pandas DataFrames
limitations: Requires a registered COSMIC account with valid email and password
Does not include local analysis, visualization, or statistical testing tools
Download speed and availability depend on COSMIC server responsiveness
Cannot access datasets behind additional paywalls or restricted-access agreements

use_cases.title: Analyze somatic mutations in a cancer cohort
use_cases.description: Download COSMIC mutation exports for a specific gene or tumor type to identify recurring somatic mutations and compare against a research study cohort.

use_cases.title: Build reproducible bioinformatics pipelines
use_cases.description: Programmatically fetch the latest COSMIC releases and Cancer Gene Census data for integration into automated genomic analysis workflows.

use_cases.title: Prepare evidence for a cancer review article
use_cases.description: Query COSMIC mutational signatures and Cancer Gene Census to gather curated evidence on known cancer driver genes for a medical literature review.

prompt_templates.title: Download mutation data for a specific gene
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the COSMIC mutation export for the TP53 gene using the cosmic-database skill. Run scripts/download_cosmic.py with my COSMIC credentials. Save the output to the current directory and confirm the file was downloaded successfully.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have COSMIC credentials and need somatic mutation data for a specific cancer gene.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch Cancer Gene Census list
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the Cancer Gene Census list from the COSMIC database. Filter for tumor suppressor genes that are associated with breast cancer. Present the results as a table with gene symbol, role in cancer, and tumor type.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a curated list of cancer-related genes for a research project or grant proposal.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve and analyze mutational signatures
prompt_templates.prompt: Download SBS and DBS mutational signatures from COSMIC. Load the data into pandas and compare the signature profiles for colorectal cancer samples. Create a summary of the most prevalent signatures found.
prompt_templates.scenario: You are conducting a mutational signature analysis study and need COSMIC reference signatures.

prompt_templates.title: Build a multi-omics COSMIC analysis pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Access COSMIC to download mutation, copy number, and fusion datasets for GRCh38 genome build. Load each dataset into a pandas DataFrame. Identify genes that show both mutation and copy number alterations. Return a combined report of overlapping findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to integrate multiple COSMIC dataset types for a comprehensive multi-omics analysis.

output_examples.input: Download COSMIC mutation data for the TP53 gene.
output_examples.output: The file CosmicMutantExport_TP53.tsv.gz has been downloaded and saved. It contains 1,847 mutation records with columns including Gene name, Accession number, Sample name, Tumor type, Mutation description, and Genome position. Load with pandas.read_csv using tab separator and gzip compression.

output_examples.input: Query the Cancer Gene Census for breast cancer associated genes.
output_examples.output: The Cancer Gene Census returned 42 genes associated with breast cancer. This includes 28 tumor suppressor genes such as BRCA1, BRCA2, and TP53, and 14 oncogenes such as ERBB2, PIK3CA, and MYC. The data includes gene symbols, roles in cancer, and associated tumor types.

best_practices: Verify that COSMIC credentials are active before starting a download session to avoid partial downloads.
Use the GRCh38 genome build for all new projects as it is the most current and recommended COSMIC reference.
Always specify output directory and confirm disk space availability before downloading large COSMIC datasets.
anti_patterns: Hardcoding COSMIC email and password directly in scripts or notebooks instead of using environment variables or interactive input.
Downloading entire COSMIC exports without filtering by gene, tumor type, or genome region when only a subset of data is required.
Assuming COSMIC file paths and API endpoints remain unchanged across releases without checking current COSMIC documentation.

faq.question: Do I need a COSMIC account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, COSMIC requires a registered account with valid email and password to download data files.

faq.question: What data formats does COSMIC provide?
faq.answer: COSMIC exports are typically GZIP-compressed TSV files and sometimes VCF format for variant data.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without internet access?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires an active internet connection to download data from the COSMIC server.

faq.question: What is the Cancer Gene Census?
faq.answer: The Cancer Gene Census is a curated catalog of genes with published evidence of involvement in cancer development.

faq.question: What mutational signature types are available?
faq.answer: COSMIC provides Single Base Substitution (SBS), Double Base Substitution (DBS), and Insertion and Deletion (ID) signatures.

faq.question: Does this skill store my COSMIC password?
faq.answer: No, credentials are passed directly to the COSMIC server for authentication and are not stored by this skill.
